accountManagement.description: Manage accounts, balances, API keys, and configure sorting priorities in the dedicated management console.
accountManagement.title: Account Management

actionClick.actionIconClickDesc: Choose open behavior when extension icon is clicked
actionClick.actionIconClickTitle: Opens when extension icon is clicked
actionClick.description: Choose what happens when you click the extension icon.
actionClick.sidepanelFallbackToast: Side panel preference saved. This device will fall back to opening the popup.
actionClick.sidepanelUnsupportedHelper: Side panel is not supported on this device. Toolbar icon clicks will fall back to opening the popup.
actionClick.title: Toolbar icon behavior

appearanceLanguage.onboardingHelper: Update the onboarding text immediately and keep this language for later settings pages.

autoProvisionKeyOnAccountAdd.description: Control whether All API Hub automatically provisions a default API key after you add an account.
autoProvisionKeyOnAccountAdd.title: API keys
autoProvisionKeyOnAccountAdd.toggleDesc: When enabled, All API Hub checks your token inventory after saving an account and creates a default token if none exist.
autoProvisionKeyOnAccountAdd.toggleLabel: Auto-create a default key on account add

changelogOnUpdate.description: Control whether All API Hub shows a What's New dialog the first time you open the extension UI after updates.
changelogOnUpdate.title: Changelog
changelogOnUpdate.toggleDesc: When enabled, the update log is shown inline on first UI open after an update (once per version). You can still open the full docs changelog manually.

claudeCodeRouter.description: Configure connection to Claude Code Router admin API
claudeCodeRouter.title: Claude Code Router
claudeCodeRouter.urlDesc: Claude Code Router server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:3000

cliProxy.checkConnectionDesc: After saving, verify that the current URL and management key can reach the CLIProxyAPI Management API.
cliProxy.description: Configure connection to CLIProxyAPI Management API
cliProxy.keyDesc: Management key for interface access authentication
cliProxy.title: CLIProxyAPI Settings
cliProxy.urlDesc: Set the Management API base URL, e.g. http://localhost:8317/v0/management

danger.resetDesc: Reset all configurations to default values, this operation cannot be undone
danger.title: Danger Zone

importExport.description: Access the dedicated page for importing and exporting data.
importExport.info: The Import/Export page provides full access to data import, exporting to different formats, and backup management.
importExport.title: Import & Export

description: Manage the basic configuration options of the plugin

display.currencyDesc: Set the default currency unit for balance and cashflow display
display.defaultTabDesc: Set the default tab displayed when the plugin starts
display.description: Customize the appearance and display preferences of the plugin
display.title: Display Settings
display.todayCashflowEnabledDesc: Controls whether today's consumption/income stats are displayed and fetched. When disabled, today stats are hidden and refresh skips the data requests used to compute them.

adminCredentialsLink.description: Open the profile page to find the system access token and user ID.
adminCredentialsLink.title: Quick access

doneHub.description: Configure integration settings for connecting to Done Hub compatible services.
doneHub.title: Done Hub Integration Settings
doneHub.tokenDesc: System access token for the administrator user. Find it in Done Hub dashboard → Personal settings → Token.
doneHub.urlDesc: Set the base URL for Done Hub integration
doneHub.userIdDesc: Administrator user ID (numeric only). Root admin is usually 1.

duplicateAccountWarningOnAdd.description: Control whether All API Hub asks for confirmation when you add an account for a site that already exists.
duplicateAccountWarningOnAdd.title: Accounts
duplicateAccountWarningOnAdd.toggleDesc: When enabled, All API Hub shows a confirmation dialog if you try to add another account for the same site URL.

logging.consoleEnabledDesc: When disabled, All API Hub will not emit any console output at any level (including errors).
logging.description: Control console logging for troubleshooting and diagnostics.
logging.minLevelDesc: When console logging is enabled, logs below this level are suppressed.
logging.title: Logging

managedSite.description: Select which API aggregator platform you want to manage. This determines which configuration will be used for channel management and model syncing.
managedSite.siteTypeDesc: Choose the platform used for channel management and model sync
managedSite.title: Managed Site Configuration

messages.resetConfirmDesc: Are you sure you want to reset {{name}} to default settings? This operation cannot be undone.
messages.resetSuccess: {{name}} has been reset to default settings

adminCredentialsLink.description: Open the admin personal settings page to find the system access token and user ID.
adminCredentialsLink.title: Quick access

newApi.description: Configure integration settings for connecting to New API compatible services.

fields.passwordDesc: Optional. Used together with the login username to sign in to the New API admin console and establish a login session when viewing a channel key or running full key comparison.
fields.totpSecretDesc: Optional. When provided, All API Hub can automatically generate TOTP codes for login 2FA and the secure verification required to view channel keys.
fields.usernameDesc: Optional. Used to sign in to the New API admin console and complete 2FA when needed. This is mainly used for viewing the real channel key in Channel Management and for checking whether a key is already used by a channel in Key Management.

sessionTest.description: Test whether the entered login username, password, and TOTP secret are correct and usable. After a successful check, you can continue with actions that require login and 2FA.
sessionTest.title: Test login & 2FA config

newApi.title: New API Integration Settings
newApi.tokenDesc: System access token for the administrator user
newApi.urlDesc: Set the base URL for New API integration

octopus.description: Configure integration settings for connecting to Octopus self-hosted sites. The system will automatically log in using your credentials and manage the JWT Token.

fields.baseUrlDesc: Set the base URL for Octopus site
fields.passwordDesc: Octopus admin password for automatic login
fields.usernameDesc: Octopus admin username for automatic login

octopus.title: Octopus Integration Settings

validation.description: Test if username and password are correct
validation.failed: Configuration validation failed, please check username and password
validation.missingFields: Please fill in all required fields
validation.success: Configuration validated successfully, connection is working
validation.title: Validate Configuration

permissions.description: Only grant these optional capabilities when you need All API Hub to handle cookies, headers, or temp-window bypass flows for you.
permissions.helper: Once enabled, the extension simply attaches your existing session cookies and headers in the background. You can disable any permission later without affecting core features.

clipboardRead.description: Allow the redemption assistant to detect copied redemption codes, thereby prompting automatic redemption of the codes.
clipboardRead.title: Clipboard Read

cookies.description: Allows All API Hub to read the necessary cookies for your connected accounts so authentication headers can be added when required.
cookies.title: Cookies

declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess.description: Allow applying request header rules by pressing Tab during the temporary window shield, attaching the correct cookies for different accounts, and avoiding data anomalies under multiple accounts.
declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess.title: Declarative Net Request

webRequest.description: Lets the extension observe outgoing requests only when temp-window bypass or auto-refresh needs to add required headers.
webRequest.title: Web Request

webRequestBlocking.description: Allows synchronous modification so cookies can be attached before a request leaves the browser, keeping firewall-bypass flows reliable.
webRequestBlocking.title: Web Request Blocking

messages.granted: {{name}} enabled. Related workflows will now run as expected.
messages.grantFailed: Couldn’t enable {{name}}. Please retry later if you still need it.

permissions.statusCaption: Permission status is synced with the browser. Click refresh if you changed permissions outside this page.
permissions.title: Permissions

permissionsOnboarding.intro: As an open-source project, we only ask for the minimal optional permissions needed to enable advanced workflows. You stay in full control.
permissionsOnboarding.permissionListDescription: These permissions are required for firewall-bypass flows so data refresh jobs keep working on protected sites.

project.cta: Go to the repository to follow versions, submit issues, or participate in development.
project.label: Project
project.starCta: Star us on GitHub to support the project!

reason.newPermissions: We added new optional permissions to support recent features. Please review and confirm to keep everything working.

permissionsOnboarding.subtitle: This open-source extension needs a few optional permissions to enable features like the temp-window bypass and other advanced workflows.
permissionsOnboarding.title: Welcome to All API Hub

refresh.description: Configure auto-refresh related settings, such as refresh interval and behavior at startup
refresh.minRefreshIntervalDesc: Minimum time interval between refreshes to avoid frequent requests (at least {{minSeconds}} seconds)
refresh.minRefreshIntervalInvalid: Please enter a valid minimum refresh interval (at least {{minSeconds}} seconds).
refresh.refreshIntervalDesc: Set the time interval for auto-refresh (default {{defaultInterval}} seconds, it is recommended not to set too small to avoid frequent requests)
refresh.refreshIntervalInvalid: Please enter a valid refresh interval (at least {{minSeconds}} seconds).
refresh.refreshOnOpenDesc: Auto-refresh account data when opening the plugin popup
refresh.shieldContextsDesc: Choose where the temp-window protection bypass is allowed. Individual options are ignored when the main switch is disabled.
refresh.shieldContextsTitle: Use protection bypass in these contexts
refresh.shieldDescriptionTempWindowOnly: When enabled, a temporary popup window may open briefly to bypass website protection pages (e.g., Cloudflare's 5-second shield) before the extension retrieves data.
refresh.shieldDescriptionWithCookieInterceptor: On Firefox, a temporary popup window may open briefly to pass protection pages and obtain cookies. The Cookie Interceptor then attaches those cookies to requests.
refresh.shieldEnabledDescTempWindowOnly: When disabled, the extension will not open the temporary popup window to bypass website protection pages.
refresh.shieldEnabledDescWithCookieInterceptor: When disabled, Firefox will not open the temporary popup window to obtain cookies for Cookie Interceptor based requests.
refresh.shieldMethodDesc: Choose which temporary context to use when running the protection bypass.
refresh.shieldMethodHintComposite: Uses a shared window and opens each bypass run as a tab inside it.
refresh.shieldMethodHintTab: Use the background tab of the current window to complete the shield, with less interface interference.
refresh.shieldMethodHintWindow: Use multiple new windows to complete the shield; a new window will pop up each time the shield is completed.
refresh.shieldPermissionWarningDesc: Grant the Cookies/Web Request permissions so the temporary window bypass can attach authenticated cookies before sending requests.
refresh.shieldPopupFirefoxNote: In Firefox popup, shielded data requests are always disabled to avoid closing the popup. This setting is still saved for other browsers.
refresh.title: Refresh Settings

helpers.checkFailed: Try again later, or open the release page to verify the latest version manually.
helpers.manualUpdate: This install is updated manually. Open the release page to get the new version.
helpers.manualUpdateGeneric: Update through your current install channel, or open the release page if you need to update manually.
helpers.notChecked: Run “Check now” to confirm whether your current version needs an update.
helpers.openLatest: Open the release page to review the latest stable release and release notes.
helpers.storeUpdate: This install usually updates automatically. You can also check the store or release page for the newest version.
helpers.unavailable: Try again later, or open the release page to review the latest stable release directly.
helpers.upToDate: Open the release page if you want to review what changed in this version.

reasons.api-unavailable: This browser environment does not support automatic detection. You can still check the latest stable release manually
reasons.chromium-development: This install channel can check the latest stable release. Run a manual check to compare versions
reasons.firefox-ambiguous: Firefox cannot reliably determine the install channel right now. Manually check the latest stable release when needed
reasons.safari-unsupported: Safari does not support automatic detection yet. Open the release page to review the latest stable release
reasons.store-build: This install channel does not support automatic detection, but you can still check the latest stable release manually
reasons.unknown: This install channel does not currently support automatic detection. You can still check the latest stable release manually

states.checkFailed: Check failed, so the latest stable release could not be retrieved
states.notChecked: The latest stable release has not been checked yet. Run a manual check to compare versions
states.updateAvailable: A newer version is available to upgrade
states.upToDate: Check complete. Your current version is already the latest stable release

sorting.checkInDesc: Place sites that need check-in at the front
sorting.currentSiteDesc: Place the currently accessed site at the top of the list
sorting.customCheckInDesc: Place sites using external check-in links at the front
sorting.customRedeemDesc: Place sites using custom redeem links at the front
sorting.customSortDesc: Sort according to the field (such as balance) selected by the user in the list view
sorting.description: Define the sorting priority of accounts in the list to organize the display of accounts
sorting.disabledAccountDesc: Disabled accounts will be uniformly moved to the bottom of the list
sorting.healthDesc: Sort by site health status (Error > Warning > Unknown > Healthy)
sorting.manualOrderDesc: Sort accounts according to your manually defined list order
sorting.matchedOpenTabsDesc: Place sites that match any open tab (by title or URL) at the front
sorting.pinnedDesc: Place pinned accounts at the top of the list in the order they were pinned
sorting.title: Sorting Priority Settings

theme.toggle: Theme toggle, current {{currentMode}} mode, click to switch to {{nextMode}} mode

title: Settings

adminCredentialsLink.description: Open the admin personal settings page to find the system access token and user ID.
adminCredentialsLink.title: Quick access

veloera.description: Configure integration settings for connecting to Veloera compatible services.
veloera.title: Veloera Integration Settings
veloera.tokenDesc: System access token for the administrator user
veloera.urlDesc: Set the base URL for Veloera integration
